sprint_meta.plan: Sovereign bug report: hero chat hangs, expects gpt-5-nano

r1_close.pm_status_summary: WK136 CLOSED (pending final qa_smoke). Root cause 1 (the hang): _poll_conversation_response only ran inside the sim-tick body; chat is allowed while PAUSED (and pause is exactly when players chat), so responses queued forever. Fix: poll once per RENDER frame in lifecycle.tick_simulation (both frontends drive it every frame regardless of pause). Root cause 2 (gpt-5-nano): 600 max_completion_tokens with no reasoning_effort -> reasoning ate the budget -> empty content -> canned fallback every time; plus 5-8s timeouts too tight. Fix: gpt-5* ladder (2000 tokens + reasoning_effort minimal->low->none), no temperature, CONVERSATION_TIMEOUT=30s (autonomous stays 5s), '<Hero> is thinking...' pending indicator. .env OPENAI_MODEL -> gpt-5-nano. Pre-fix test failures proved both bugs; real-key E2E through a PAUSED engine: 2.4-2.5s, real in-character responses delivered to ChatPanel.

evidence.new_tests: tests/test_wk136_chat_delivery.py (13, real engine loop, 6 failed pre-fix)

Seed alternating warrior/ranger heroes near castle (mimic _seed_digest_heroes; keep direct refs — permadeath culling removes dead heroes from engine lists). Snapshot WaveEventSystem._active_wave_enemies after _pending_spawns drains. Matrix seeds [11,23,37,41,53,67,71,83,97,101] x H in (10,8,6); H=8 sweep KINGDOM_INITIAL_WAVE_GOBLINS 8..14 via subprocess (env before config import). Bands (NORMAL): H=10 wins>=9/10 AND mean deaths<=1.5; H=8 wins>=7/10 AND mean deaths in [1.0,4.5]; H=6 wins<=6/10 OR mean deaths>=3.5. Pytest = 3-seed subset, deterministic, slow-marked per repo convention. Plus ONE realistic full-systems sanity run (spawner+lairs ON, H=8, seed 11, 180 sim-sec, no-crash + wave fires). Gates: digest, full suite, qa_smoke --quick. Verdict line: 'BALANCE: PASS at goblin_count=N' or 'FAIL — recommend goblin_count=M' (r2 = 05 one-constant retune, you re-run). Lanes: tools/wk137_balance_probe.py + tests/test_wk137_initial_wave_balance.py ONLY — you do NOT edit config.py. DO NOT COMMIT (PM commits).

pm_send_list_minimal.rationale: Two disjoint wave-1 lanes then QA: 05 high (digest-adjacent sim change + spec-derived stats + table-shift trap), 03 high (hot-loop renderer change + atlas pre-existing-bug fix across two render paths), 11 high (deterministic multi-seed balance harness + tuning recommendation drives the close). No UI agent: HUD events already wired (wave_incoming/hud_message). No art agent: warchief reuses goblin PNGs via alias. 10's perf concern bounded to one multiply in the enemy loop, folded into 03's constraint + PM capture review. r2 conditional: 05 retunes goblin_count to 11's recommendation if BALANCE: FAIL.

r2_clustered_spawn_retune.pm_status_summary: r1 RESULTS: 05 done (8 tests, digest green, suite 1854, engine smoke: 1 warchief + goblins at 35 sim-sec; + mandatory test_wk73_enemy_stats key-pin extension mirroring the dragon precedent). 03 done (per-size scale both renderers, atlas packs 4 boss types fixing pre-existing wrong-fallback-sprite bug, name labels prefer .name incl. instanced_unit_labels.py; 31 wk137+digest passed, 44 renderer parity passed, import seam OK). 11 done with a STRUCTURAL FINDING: BALANCE FAIL at H=8/H=6 — across the ENTIRE count sweep 8..14, mean hero deaths never exceed 0.4 and H=6 wins 10/10 (bands: H=8 needs deaths in [1.0,4.5]; H=6 needs <=6/10 wins OR deaths>=3.5). H=10 band PASSES (9/10 wins, 0 deaths). Root cause is engagement geometry, NOT stats: _edge_position draws a fresh random position along the whole map edge PER ENEMY, so the wave arrives as a strung-out trickle; rangers out-range goblins and kite; heroes mop up at full HP. The count lever is insufficient — r2 is a structural fix: spawn the INITIAL wave as a tight cluster around a single edge anchor so it engages as a pack and focus-fires the melee line. 11's harness encodes H8/H6 bands as xfail(strict=False) markers (suite green: 1860 passed, 0 failed) to be hardened after retune. Realistic sanity PASS (wave at 30.0s, warband cleared, table Goblin Raid unshifted at 110s). Digest byte-identical throughout.

pm_next_actions_by_agent.05_GameplaySystems: Clustered spawn for the initial wave ONLY (table waves byte-identical): single rng-chosen edge anchor + small per-enemy jitter, plus a diagnostic pass on why heroes take ~zero damage (check the has_attackers attacker-slot cap on focus fire). Then re-tune goblin_count default if 11's clustered re-probe recommends it. Full dispatch prompt from PM; lanes config.py + wave_events.py + tests/test_wk137_initial_wave.py ONLY. Boss stat multipliers (2x hp / 1.5x attack vs goblin) are FIXED by Sovereign spec — never tune those; 1 warchief on NORMAL stays.
pm_next_actions_by_agent.11_QA: Wave 2 of r2: re-run the full matrix + count sweep against the clustered spawn; deliver BALANCE verdict; if PASS, harden the H8/H6 xfail markers into real asserts at the passing count.

r3_spawn_distance.pm_status_summary: r2 RESULTS: 05 done — clustered_edge direction shipped (wave spread 222.9 tiles -> ~4 tiles, jitter knob KINGDOM_INITIAL_WAVE_JITTER=2), Part-A diagnostics with data: wave targets HEROES ~100% (no building-siege hijack; lever-4 targeting nudge correctly NOT used, enemy.py untouched), NO attacker-slot cap exists (attackers set is gold-attribution only), and the real band-blocker is hero LEVELING: rangers reach 140-160 hp (level_up = +20 max_hp + FULL HEAL, fog-reveal XP) during the ~30s the wave spends marching from the map edge, plus H=6 heroes paradoxically stay clumped/safer. Even count=20-28 cannot reach the H8/H6 death bands from edge spawns. All gates green: suite 1861 passed/0 failed (band markers XPASS on the 3-seed subset), digest byte-identical, qa_smoke PASS. PM DECISION OF RECORD (r3): the Sovereign fixed the TRIGGER TIME (30 sim-sec) but never specified the SPAWN LOCATION — map edge was a PM r1 simplification, and it makes the '30-second wave' engage at ~60s against leveled heroes. r3 lever: spawn the cluster at a configurable distance from the castle (default ~22 tiles, env KINGDOM_INITIAL_WAVE_DIST) on a deterministic random bearing, so engagement happens ~38s against still-level-1 heroes — truer to the Sovereign's 'attacks after the first 30 seconds' AND restores the regime the plan's balance math assumed (60hp/10atk heroes). Trigger stays 30s; warning stays 20s; table waves stay byte-identical; boss multipliers stay fixed.

Closing H6 needs a Sovereign lever: (a) cap pre-wave fog-reveal leveling (cleanest, attacks root cause), (b) +1 warchief on NORMAL (changes the 'a boss' spec), or (c) revise the H6 expectation. - Runtime instances should store stable ids and primitive facts, not live object refs.
- Phase history records should be small primitive dicts, e.g. {event: phase_completed, phase_id: collect_relic, hero_id: 12, time_ms: 12345}.
- QuestChainSystem.update must early-return when no chains are offered/active.
- Use sim time and existing deterministic RNG patterns. Do not introduce wall-clock time or global random.
- If a real item object is too risky for this slice, represent the relic as a quest fact first, but leave a clear path to item integration. The UI/prompt must not claim more than the sim stores.

Acceptance:
- A deterministic test completes all three phases in order and records phase history.
- Reward/completion cleanup leaves no dangling active phase.
- Empty/default path is digest-stable.
- Failure/abandon cleanup has at least a minimal test, even if richer failure branches wait for later sprints.

r1_kickoff.pm_status_summary: WK140 KICKOFF inserted by Sovereign request after WK139: before Blackbanner's Toll, fix the lame early-game hero AI where spawning about 10 heroes makes them scatter into tight local loops around town like ants. Mission: add a deterministic daily-life ambient motive layer so idle heroes visibly diversify across roaming, exploration, POI scouting, monster patrol, safe rest, social lingering, and opportunity checking. This sprint intentionally changes default autonomous behavior, so old WK67 digest byte-identity may change if the tests prove deterministic intentional behavior change; the sacred invariant is determinism, priority ordering, and AI/sim separation.

pm_feature_requests.title: Hero daily-life ambient AI variety
pm_feature_requests.player_value: Newly hired heroes should look like independent fantasy adventurers with varied daily motives, not a swarm of local-looping units.
in_scope: Ambient motive layer in base/no-LLM autonomous AI
Motive categories: kingdom_roam, wilderness_explore, poi_scout, monster_patrol, safe_rest, social_linger, opportunity_check
Per-hero anti-loop memory and target cooldowns
Spacing pressure so heroes do not all pick the same nearby cluster
Deterministic tests for 10-hero distribution, class bias, low-health safety, urgent behavior preservation
out_of_scope: LLM-only behavior that does not work in --no-llm
definition_of_done: A deterministic around-10-hero test produces at least 4 ambient motive categories in the first few in-game minutes.
No more than a small majority of heroes choose the same tiny local target cluster unless an urgent event justifies it.
Heroes can choose visible farther targets: fog/frontier, POI, enemies/lairs, and safe/social buildings.
Low-health heroes prefer safe_rest over dangerous motives.
Warriors bias toward patrol/monster work, rangers toward frontier/explore, rogues toward POI/opportunity work.
Anti-loop memory prevents immediate repeated retargeting to the same small tile/area.
Existing urgent behaviors remain intact: combat, healing, active quest continuation, boss/quest handling, shopping, accepted bounties.
Behavior is deterministic for a fixed seed.
Any WK67 digest change is explicitly explained as expected behavior change and proven stable.
python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick passes.

r1_kickoff.pm_status_summary: WK142 KICKOFF: Dynamic Situations - Rescue And Revenge. Mission: turn boss/POI failure into playable story hooks. This sprint builds one bounded Blackbanner/Bandit Fortress capture/rescue loop and one named-boss revenge loop, then exposes those facts to AI, Adventure Ledger/UI, and visual proof. It also continues the user's feedback that current heroes feel less alive than v1.4-era AI by giving heroes stronger world-scale reasons to roam, scout POIs, seek monsters/bosses, rest, visit castle/Herald's Post, and choose varied rescue/revenge behaviors. Prior work: WK138 QuestChainSystem, WK139 BossEncounterSystem, WK140 Daily Life AI, WK141 Blackbanner's Toll all shipped/pushed. Use gpt-5.4-mini with xhigh effort for active subagents.

pm_feature_requests.title: Blackbanner Captives rescue loop
pm_feature_requests.player_value: When a hero is captured by Blackbanner/Bandit Fortress danger, the kingdom can post a real rescue opportunity and another hero can free them.
definition_of_done: A deterministic test creates a captured Blackbanner captive and completes rescue.
Captured heroes are unavailable for normal AI/task routing until rescued.
Adventure Ledger/UI shows captive, captor, target, current phase, and rescued completion state.
AI sees rescue facts from structured view only and can choose varied rescue/rest/scout behaviors without all heroes stampeding.
Duplicate or stale rescue offers are suppressed.

pm_feature_requests.title: Named boss revenge loop
pm_feature_requests.player_value: If a named boss kills a hero, the kingdom remembers it and can surface a revenge quest against the actual boss.
definition_of_done: A deterministic test records named boss kills hero -> revenge situation/chain offer.
Revenge chain points to the real boss and fallen hero and completes or expires cleanly.
LLM/prompt context states boss kill memory only from structured facts.
UI/visuals make the revenge target readable enough for the player.
Duplicate revenge offers for the same boss/fallen hero pair are suppressed.

pm_feature_requests.title: More robust day-to-day hero AI around story hooks
pm_feature_requests.player_value: At 10-hero startup and during active world events, heroes should no longer look like a cluster of tiny-loop wanderers near town.
definition_of_done: Tests prove heroes distribute across safe roam, POI exploration, monster/boss pressure, rescue/revenge consideration, rest/home, and castle/Herald's Post visit intentions when facts support those choices.
Urgent survival/retreat gates remain higher priority than ambition.
Captured heroes do not choose ordinary daily-life tasks.

definition_of_done: A deterministic test completes Ashwing's Hoard through scout/prep/slay/claim.
Ashwing is a named boss using BossEncounterSystem, not a one-off stat rename.
Dragon fire telegraphs before impact/damage.
Victory grants hoard reward, legendary loot, and a hero title/memory.
AI sees dragon/prep/danger facts from structured context only and preserves retreat/survival gates.
Adventure Ledger/HUD, visuals, audio, screenshots, WK67, validate_assets if applicable, and qa_smoke are green.

pm_feature_requests.title: Dragon Hunt showcase capture and proof

definition_of_done: A `dragon_hunt_showcase` capture scenario exists or a documented fallback is used.
Screenshots under docs/screenshots/wk143_* are inspected, not merely generated.
Fire telegraph and boss identity are readable in proof captures.

r5_final_qa_raid_lair_repair.pm_status_summary: WK143 final QA found one blocking full-smoke regression outside the dragon feature surface: tests/test_wk126_quest_soak.py::test_soak_all_four_quest_types_complete_and_pay fails because raid_lair quests fail with reason hero_died before all four quest types complete. WK143 focused tests, WK139/WK141/WK142 regressions, WK67, validate_assets, peer log validation, and screenshot inspection were otherwise green. Repair is assigned to Agent 05 because raid_lair completion/survival lives in gameplay systems and entities.
pm_blocker_evidence: Agent 11 final QA: python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick -> FAIL: 1 failed, 1972 passed, 5 skipped, 1 xfailed; failing test tests/test_wk126_quest_soak.py::test_soak_all_four_quest_types_complete_and_pay.

PM reproduced: python -m pytest tests/test_wk126_quest_soak.py::test_soak_all_four_quest_types_complete_and_pay -q -> FAIL with completed types {'explore_far', 'find_poi', 'slay_enemy_type'} and failed raid_lair quests with reason hero_died.

The WK126 soak intentionally drives raid_lair after acceptance by moving the accepting hero adjacent, setting the lair to low HP, topping the hero to max HP, weakening nearby defenders, and repinning the lair target when combat detours clear it. The desired fix is to restore the real-combat LAIR_CLEARED -> QuestSystem.on_lair_cleared completion path, not to weaken the test contract unless the production bug proves impossible.

pm_agent_prompts.05: Task: WK143 final QA repair - restore WK126 raid_lair soak completion. Onboard as Agent 05 by reading AGENTS.md and your .cursor/rules role instructions. Then read .cursor/plans/agent_logs/agent_01_ExecutiveProducer_PM.json -> sprints["wk143_dragon_hunt_showcase"].rounds["r5_final_qa_raid_lair_repair"], .cursor/plans/wk143_dragon_hunt_showcase.plan.md, and tests/test_wk126_quest_soak.py. Problem: WK143 is otherwise green, but full qa_smoke fails because tests/test_wk126_quest_soak.py::test_soak_all_four_quest_types_complete_and_pay no longer completes raid_lair; two raid_lair quests fail with reason hero_died. Preserve the soak's intent: a real engine run, real combat, lair cleared through LAIR_CLEARED -> QuestSystem.on_lair_cleared, and payout through QuestSystem._complete. Do not paper over the failure by deleting the raid_lair objective, relaxing the all-four-types assertion, force-completing quests in the test, or globally making heroes immortal. Diagnose why the existing raid_lair drive no longer keeps the accepting hero alive/targeting long enough to land the lair clear. Likely areas are game/systems/quest.py, game/systems/combat.py, game/entities/hero.py, and any WK143 gameplay changes in game/systems/quest_chain.py or game/systems/boss_encounter.py that accidentally affect ordinary lair raids. MAY edit game/entities/**, game/systems/**, game/content/** only if needed for gameplay data, gameplay tests, and your own log. MUST NOT edit ai/**, game/ui/**, game/graphics/**, game/audio/**, tools/**, assets/**, config.py, README, CHANGELOG, or .cursor/plans/** except your Agent 05 log. Verification required from repo root PowerShell: (1) python -m pytest tests/test_wk126_quest_soak.py::test_soak_all_four_quest_types_complete_and_pay -q, (2) python -m pytest tests/test_wk143_dragon_hunt.py tests/test_wk143_dragon_fire_telegraph.py tests/test_wk143_dragon_rewards.py -q, (3) python -m pytest tests/test_wk139_boss_encounters.py tests/test_wk141_blackbanner_chain.py tests/test_wk141_blackbanner_boss.py tests/test_wk142_dynamic_rescue_gameplay.py tests/test_wk142_boss_revenge.py -q, (4) python -m pytest tests/test_wk67_ai_boundary.py -q.
